run_summary: Portfolio down -1.94% (AEIS) and -3.64% (TXRH) after 0-1 day hold; both theses remain intact but neither warrants action yet. New flag pool is dominated by rational earnings reactions with appropriate magnitudes; AEIS reappears as OVERDONE conf 3 with better-articulated thesis (annual meeting false signal), worth reconsidering. Most new flags are SKIP or WATCH — they're correctly priced on available info, and conviction-tier entries require high-confidence reversal reads that this batch doesn't generate.

entries.name: Ducommun Incorporated
entries.thesis: Move magnitude is consistent with an earnings beat on a $2.3B aerospace/defense supplier. 2x volume and +4.2% in a flat-to-down defense tape suggests company-specific beat rather than sector lift. Without seeing the actual print details I can't say whether it's overdone or underdone — but the magnitude is in the normal range for an earnings beat at this size.
entries.what_kills: If the print was an in-line beat with no guidance raise, +4% is the full reaction
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 with a real 8-K catalyst (Q1 earnings, 2x volume). Setup is coherent: small-cap aerospace supplier, earnings beat magnitude normal. However, flag author explicitly states 'I can't say whether it's overdone or underdone' — the thesis is that the move is *calibrated*, not misprice. Without conviction that the market has misjudged the magnitude or missed a follow-on catalyst (e.g., guide raise buried in exhibits), there's no asymmetry to exploit. The 'what_confirms' and 'what_kills' are about the earnings quality, not the price reaction itself. This is a rational discovery, not a trading opportunity.

entries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.

Combined with the 5/8 material agreement filing (item 1.01 + 1.02), there's a sequence of business development activity. +4% on average volume is a measured reaction — neither overdone nor underdone without knowing the specific announcement content.

The 5/13 8-K item 8.01 ('other events') is the proximate driver, but the flag author notes +4% is 'neither overdone nor underdone without knowing the specific announcement content.' This is a wait-and-see flag, not a discovery. Until we know whether the 8.01 is a clinical win (positive confirmation) or a procedural re-filing, there's no edge. The biotech structure is known; the catalyst is opaque. Skip pending disclosure details.

No evidence that -9% cumulative is excessive relative to met coal weakness and soft earnings. Skip unless met coal benchmarks bounce.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Semi-cap sympathy with SMH -2.6%; May 8 8-K likely annual meeting results
entries.thesis: AEIS down 4.4% on below-average volume aligns more with SMH -2.6% sympathy than with the May 8 8-K, which contained 5.02/5.03/5.07/9.01 — the combination strongly suggests this was a routine annual-meeting filing (5.07 is shareholder vote results, 5.03 is charter amendment, 5.02 here likely director elections, 9.01 boilerplate), not a sudden departure. If the 8-K were materially bad it would have hit on May 8, not 4 days later on light volume. The semi-equipment beta drag explains most of today's move; magnitude looks extended versus the actual catalyst chain.
entries.what_kills: If the 5.02 item actually documents a CFO/CEO departure with no successor named, the governance overhang is real and -4% is justified or insufficient; if SMH keeps falling, sympathy drag continues regardless of AEIS fundamentals.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The bull thesis assumes routine annual-meeting filings fade quickly via mean reversion, but provides no evidence that semi-cap sympathy selloffs on 'light volume' actually reverse faster than the broader SMH decline that triggered them. Bear case: The bull case rests on two untested premises: (1) the 8-K is definitely routine governance (5.02/5.03/5.07/9.01 bundle), and (2) if routine, AEIS will mean-revert faster than peers LRCX/AMAT/ACLS over 1-3 days. However, the flag itself acknowledges that the -4.38% move aligns 'more with SMH -2.6% sympathy than with the May 8 8-K'—meaning the 8-K timing and the selloff timing don't match. If sympathy is the true driver, AEIS won't outperform until SMH stabilizes; the exploratory position sizes on the assumption of a fade that depends on external (SMH) rather than company-specific (AEIS governance clarity) catalysts. The confidence level (3/5) already signals weak conviction, yet the trade is being added to at 6% notional exposure. Adding to a sympathy play with no AEIS-specific reversal trigger is betting on relative strength in a down market, not on a discrete catalyst. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 reappearance with a much sharper thesis than original entry. The flag now articulates a specific 8-K misread: the May 8 filing combined 5.02 + 5.03 + 5.07 + 9.01, which is a classic annual-meeting-results bundle (director elections, charter amendments, shareholder vote results, boilerplate). The sympathy with SMH -2.6% likely overweights this routine governance event. If AEIS means-reverts faster than semi-equipment peers (LRCX, AMAT, ACLS) over 1-3 sessions *without fresh news*, the setup is confirmed. Currently holding 1 share at -1.94%; adding 1 more at exploratory (6% = ~$600 / $344.60 ≈ 1.7 shares, round to 1 share) would bring position to 2 shares (~1.2% of portfolio by cost basis). Conviction is only conf 3 (not 4-5), so exploratory tier is appropriate. Will learn whether the routine-8K-sympathy-fade thesis works.)

red_team.weakest_link: The bull thesis assumes routine annual-meeting filings fade quickly via mean reversion, but provides no evidence that semi-cap sympathy selloffs on 'light volume' actually reverse faster than the broader SMH decline that triggered them.
red_team.critique: The bull case rests on two untested premises: (1) the 8-K is definitely routine governance (5.02/5.03/5.07/9.01 bundle), and (2) if routine, AEIS will mean-revert faster than peers LRCX/AMAT/ACLS over 1-3 days. However, the flag itself acknowledges that the -4.38% move aligns 'more with SMH -2.6% sympathy than with the May 8 8-K'—meaning the 8-K timing and the selloff timing don't match. If sympathy is the true driver, AEIS won't outperform until SMH stabilizes; the exploratory position sizes on the assumption of a fade that depends on external (SMH) rather than company-specific (AEIS governance clarity) catalysts. The confidence level (3/5) already signals weak conviction, yet the trade is being added to at 6% notional exposure. Adding to a sympathy play with no AEIS-specific reversal trigger is betting on relative strength in a down market, not on a discrete catalyst.

entries.name: Shake Shack, Inc.

The combination of an earnings print AND a management change in the same filing window is a textbook bad-news bundle. With the stock already down 32.5% over 5 days, today's -8% likely reflects continued institutional re-rating rather than fresh news. Magnitude appears justified given dual catalysts.
entries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be planned board succession or a controlled CEO transition with a strong replacement named — then the -32% 5-day move is overdone and a bounce candidate.
entries.reasoning: LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4, down -8.04% on top of -32.5% 5-day move. The thesis is correct: earnings + 5.02 officer departure in the same window is a dual-catalyst bad-news bundle, magnitude is *justified*. The flag author notes the move 'likely reflects continued institutional re-rating rather than fresh news.' No mispricing signal — SKIP.

Flag notes that 'quality compounders moving sharply on earnings need granular segment analysis before fading — IBP-class names often present asymmetric entries 5-10 trading days post-print.' This is a *potential* fade candidate if housing data stabilizes, but it requires deeper work (transcript analysis, builder concentration, margin trends). Mark WATCH; reassess if housing data shows stabilization or insider buying emerges in next 2-5 days.

This is rational commodity beta with a recent positive print as the floor.

However, flag notes 'HL 5-day gain is already +17.6%, so positioning getting crowded.' This is a *momo* continuation trade, not a discovery of mispricing. The what_kills (silver reversal on profit-taking, crowded positioning) is now the higher-probability outcome after a 5-day +17.6% run. Skip.

Flag notes 'momentum is extended.' The +25% 5-day move has likely captured the beta to OpenAI/AI narrative; today's +5% is trailing entry, not a discovery. entries.name: Kodiak Gas Services

Underperforming XLU (+0.94%) on earnings day suggests real liability re-rating rather than soft earnings alone. The flag author notes 'earnings likely surfaced reserve/liability commentary that re-rates the credit.' What_kills (pure soft EPS with no incremental litigation news, bounces in days) is a clear path to a reversal, but requires confirmation from the 8-K exhibits. Mark WATCH; if the exhibits show *no* expanded reserves disclosure, this becomes a fade candidate (UNDERDONE) in 2-3 sessions.

entries.name: Fluor Corporation
entries.thesis: Fluor is down 15% on Q1 print; CNBC framed it as a pullback opportunity, suggesting the print wasn't a thesis-breaker but rather a guide/timing miss typical of E&C. Five-day -18% means most of the damage is already in. Engineering & construction earnings frequently swing on project timing and one-offs (NuScale stake mark, segment margins) rather than secular issues. The 8-K is just 2.02 + 5.07 (annual meeting vote) — no impairment, no restructuring, no management change.

entries.reasoning: PARTIALLY RATIONAL conf 3, down -15.21%. Engineering & construction earnings drop is severe, but the flag author notes the 8-K contains only 2.02 + 5.07 — *no impairment, no restructuring, no management change*. CNBC framed it as a 'pullback opportunity.' The 5-day -18% means most damage is in. The what_confirms (backlog intact, miss tied to project timing/NuScale mark) is a plausible fade setup *if* the transcript confirms it. Mark WATCH; if backlog and NuScale guidance are intact, this becomes an UNDERDONE conf 3-4 candidate for entry in 1-2 days post-call.

entries.name: Oshkosh Corporation
entries.thesis: OSK -9.6% on Q1 print with only 2.02 + 5.07 in the 8-K (no impairment, no management change). Coming on the same day as Trump's renewed EU tariff threats, an industrial with European exposure and access-equipment cyclicality is a natural target. Cumulative -10.9% over 5 days. Without granular guide info, hard to say if 9.6% is calibrated, but absence of structural codes suggests it's a guide/margin issue rather than a thesis-break.

entries.reasoning: PARTIALLY RATIONAL conf 3, down -9.61%. Oshkosh hit on Q1 print + Trump tariff tape. Only 2.02 + 5.07 in 8-K, no structural codes (no impairment, no management change). The -10.9% 5-day move is elevated but no evidence yet that it's overdone. Tariff fears are real but OSK's NGDV (postal vehicle) program has strong moat. What_kills (backlog reaffirmed, NGDV on track, miss tied to Q1 mix only) is a clear path to reversal. Mark WATCH; if NGDV and backlog commentary are positive, this becomes a fade candidate in 1-2 days.

entries.name: BILL Holdings, Inc.
entries.thesis: BILL is up 11% on a print that includes a 2.05 (exit/disposal costs) item. Normally 2.05 is a drag, but when paired with a positive stock reaction it usually means the market is rewarding the cost-cut/right-sizing story over the charge itself — classic 'kitchen sink + better core' setup. BILL has been a battleground/short name; a restructuring narrative could be re-rating fuel. Risk is the rally fades once people parse the charge magnitude vs. forward op-margin guide.
entries.what_kills: Restructuring charge is large with no offsetting margin commitment; revenue guide cut. In that case the 11% pop fades.

With only 2.02 in the 8-K (no impairment, no management change), the driver is likely guide cut or competitive concerns (NuVasive integration, robotics share). The cumulative 23% drawdown in a defensive medtech is more interesting than today's 8% in isolation — could be approaching washout or could mark a structural break.

entries.name: Assured Guaranty
entries.catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings + next-day Reg FD disclosure
entries.thesis: Two filings in two days (earnings 5/7, then 7.01 Reg FD on 5/8) plus a -9.75% drop is unusual for a financial guarantee insurer that typically grinds. The follow-up 7.01 the day after earnings often signals clarification or correction of guidance — worth digging into the FD disclosure.
entries.what_kills: If 7.01 is benign (investor day slides, dividend reaffirmation), -9.75% is overdone for a defensive insurer
